NKJV.1: Therefore if there is any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any affection and mercy,
NKJV.2: fulfill my joy by being like-minded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind.
NKJV.3: Let nothing be done through selfish ambition or conceit, but in lowliness of mind let each esteem others better than himself.
NKJV.4: Let each of you look out not only for his own interests, but also for the interests of others.
NKJV.5: Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus,
NKJV.6: who, being in the form of God, did not consider it robbery to be equal with God,
NKJV.7: but made Himself of no reputation, taking the form of a bondservant, and coming in the likeness of men.
NKJV.8: And being found in appearance as a man, He humbled Himself and became obedient to the point of death, even the death of the cross.
NKJV.9: Therefore God also has highly exalted Him and given Him the name which is above every name,
NKJV.10: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, and of those on earth, and of those under the earth,
NKJV.11: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
NKJV.12: Therefore, my beloved, as you have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling;
NKJV.13: for it is God who works in you both to will and to do for His good pleasure.
NKJV.14: Do all things without complaining and disputing,
NKJV.15: that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without fault in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world,
NKJV.16: holding fast the word of life, so that I may rejoice in the day of Christ that I have not run in vain or labored in vain.
NKJV.17: Yes, and if I am being poured out as a drink offering on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
NKJV.18: For the same reason you also be glad and rejoice with me.
NKJV.19: But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you shortly, that I also may be encouraged when I know your state.
NKJV.20: For I have no one like-minded, who will sincerely care for your state.
NKJV.21: For all seek their own, not the things which are of Christ Jesus.
NKJV.22: But you know his proven character, that as a son with his father he served with me in the gospel.
NKJV.23: Therefore I hope to send him at once, as soon as I see how it goes with me.
NKJV.24: But I trust in the Lord that I myself shall also come shortly.
NKJV.25: Yet I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, and fellow soldier, but your messenger and the one who ministered to my need;
NKJV.26: since he was longing for you all, and was distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
NKJV.27: For indeed he was sick almost unto death; but God had mercy on him, and not only on him but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.
NKJV.28: Therefore I sent him the more eagerly, that when you see him again you may rejoice, and I may be less sorrowful.
NKJV.29: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness, and hold such men in esteem;
NKJV.30: because for the work of Christ he came close to death, not regarding his life, to supply what was lacking in your service toward me.

ESV.1: So if there is any encouragement in Christ, any comfort from love, any participation in the Spirit, any affection and sympathy,
ESV.2: complete my joy by being of the same mind, having the same love, being in full accord and of one mind.
ESV.3: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
ESV.4: Let each of you look not only to his own interests, but also to the interests of others.
ESV.5: Have this mind among yourselves, which is yours in Christ Jesus,
ESV.6: who, though he was in the form of God, did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped,
ESV.7: but emptied himself, by taking the form of a servant,  being born in the likeness of men.
ESV.8: And being found in human form, he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
ESV.9: Therefore God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name,
ESV.10: so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
ESV.11: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
ESV.12: Therefore, my beloved, as you have always obeyed, so now, not only as in my presence but much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling,
ESV.13: for it is God who works in you, both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
ESV.14: Do all things without grumbling or disputing,
ESV.15: that you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and twisted generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world,
ESV.16: holding fast to the word of life, so that in the day of Christ I may be proud that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
ESV.17: Even if I am to be poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrificial offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
ESV.18: Likewise you also should be glad and rejoice with me.
ESV.19: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I too may be cheered by news of you.
ESV.20: For I have no one like him, who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
ESV.21: For they all seek their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
ESV.22: But you know Timothy's  proven worth, how as a son  with a father he has served with me in the gospel.
ESV.23: I hope therefore to send him just as soon as I see how it will go with me,
ESV.24: and I trust in the Lord that shortly I myself will come also.
ESV.25: I have thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier, and your messenger and minister to my need,
ESV.26: for he has been longing for you all and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
ESV.27: Indeed he was ill, near to death. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.
ESV.28: I am the more eager to send him, therefore, that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.
ESV.29: So receive him in the Lord with all joy, and honor such men,
ESV.30: for he nearly died  for the work of Christ, risking his life to complete what was lacking in your service to me.

NASB.1: Therefore if there is any encouragement in Christ, if there is any consolation of love, if there is any fellowship of the Spirit, if any affection and compassion,
NASB.2: make my joy complete by being of the same mind, maintaining the same love, united in spirit, intent on one purpose.
NASB.3: Do nothing from selfishness or empty conceit, but with humility of mind regard one another as more important than yourselves;
NASB.4: do not [merely] look out for your own personal interests, but also for the interests of others.
NASB.5: Have this attitude in yourselves which was also in Christ Jesus,
NASB.6: who, although He existed in the form of God, did not regard equality with God a thing to be grasped,
NASB.7: but emptied Himself, taking the form of a bond-servant, [and] being made in the likeness of men.
NASB.8: Being found in appearance as a man, He humbled Himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
NASB.9: For this reason also, God highly exalted Him, and bestowed on Him the name which is above every name,
NASB.10: so that at the name of Jesus EVERY KNEE WILL BOW, of those who are in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
NASB.11: and that every tongue will confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
NASB.12: So then, my beloved, just as you have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your salvation with fear and trembling;
NASB.13: for it is God who is at work in you, both to will and to work for [His] good pleasure.
NASB.14: Do all things without grumbling or disputing;
NASB.15: so that you will prove yourselves to be blameless and innocent, children of God above reproach in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you appear as lights in the world,
NASB.16: holding fast the word of life, so that in the day of Christ I will have reason to glory because I did not run in vain nor toil in vain.
NASB.17: But even if I am being poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice and share my joy with you all.
NASB.18: You too, [I urge you], rejoice in the same way and share your joy with me.
NASB.19: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you shortly, so that I also may be encouraged when I learn of your condition.
NASB.20: For I have no one [else] of kindred spirit who will genuinely be concerned for your welfare.
NASB.21: For they all seek after their own interests, not those of Christ Jesus.
NASB.22: But you know of his proven worth, that he served with me in the furtherance of the gospel like a child [serving] his father.
NASB.23: Therefore I hope to send him immediately, as soon as I see how things [go] with me;
NASB.24: and I trust in the Lord that I myself also will be coming shortly.
NASB.25: But I thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier, who is also your messenger and minister to my need;
NASB.26: because he was longing for you all and was distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
NASB.27: For indeed he was sick to the point of death, but God had mercy on him, and not on him only but also on me, so that I would not have sorrow upon sorrow.
NASB.28: Therefore I have sent him all the more eagerly so that when you see him again you may rejoice and I may be less concerned [about you].
NASB.29: Receive him then in the Lord with all joy, and hold men like him in high regard;
NASB.30: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to complete what was deficient in your service to me.

NLT.1: Is there any encouragement from belonging to Christ? Any comfort from his love? Any fellowship together in the Spirit? Are your hearts tender and compassionate?
NLT.2: Then make me truly happy by agreeing wholeheartedly with each other, loving one another, and working together with one mind and purpose.
NLT.3: Don’t be selfish; don’t try to impress others. Be humble, thinking of others as better than yourselves.
NLT.4: Don’t look out only for your own interests, but take an interest in others, too.
NLT.5: You must have the same attitude that Christ Jesus had.
NLT.6:
Though he was God,
he did not think of equality with God
as something to cling to.
NLT.7:
Instead, he gave up his divine privileges ;
he took the humble position of a slave
and was born as a human being.
When he appeared in human form,
NLT.8:
he humbled himself in obedience to God
and died a criminal’s death on a cross.
NLT.9:
Therefore, God elevated him to the place of highest honor
and gave him the name above all other names,
NLT.10:
that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow,
in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
NLT.11:
and every tongue declare that Jesus Christ is Lord,
to the glory of God the Father.
NLT.12: Dear friends, you always followed my instructions when I was with you. And now that I am away, it is even more important. Work hard to show the results of your salvation, obeying God with deep reverence and fear.
NLT.13: For God is working in you, giving you the desire and the power to do what pleases him.
NLT.14: Do everything without complaining and arguing,
NLT.15: so that no one can criticize you. Live clean, innocent lives as children of God, shining like bright lights in a world full of crooked and perverse people.
NLT.16: Hold firmly to the word of life; then, on the day of Christ’s return, I will be proud that I did not run the race in vain and that my work was not useless.
NLT.17: But I will rejoice even if I lose my life, pouring it out like a liquid offering to God, just like your faithful service is an offering to God. And I want all of you to share that joy.
NLT.18: Yes, you should rejoice, and I will share your joy.
NLT.19: If the Lord Jesus is willing, I hope to send Timothy to you soon for a visit. Then he can cheer me up by telling me how you are getting along.
NLT.20: I have no one else like Timothy, who genuinely cares about your welfare.
NLT.21: All the others care only for themselves and not for what matters to Jesus Christ.
NLT.22: But you know how Timothy has proved himself. Like a son with his father, he has served with me in preaching the Good News.
NLT.23: I hope to send him to you just as soon as I find out what is going to happen to me here.
NLT.24: And I have confidence from the Lord that I myself will come to see you soon.
NLT.25: Meanwhile, I thought I should send Epaphroditus back to you. He is a true brother, co-worker, and fellow soldier. And he was your messenger to help me in my need.
NLT.26: I am sending him because he has been longing to see you, and he was very distressed that you heard he was ill.
NLT.27: And he certainly was ill; in fact, he almost died. But God had mercy on him — and also on me, so that I would not have one sorrow after another.
NLT.28: So I am all the more anxious to send him back to you, for I know you will be glad to see him, and then I will not be so worried about you.
NLT.29: Welcome him in the Lord’s love and with great joy, and give him the honor that people like him deserve.
NLT.30: For he risked his life for the work of Christ, and he was at the point of death while doing for me what you couldn’t do from far away.

WEB.1: If there is therefore any exhortation in Christ, if any consolation of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any tender mercies and compassion,
WEB.2: make my joy full, by being like-minded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind;
WEB.3: doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself;
WEB.4: each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.
WEB.5: Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus,
WEB.6: who, existing in the form of God, didn’t consider equality with God a thing to be grasped,
WEB.7: but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men.
WEB.8: And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, yes, the death of the cross.
WEB.9: Therefore God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name;
WEB.10: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, those on earth, and those under the earth,
WEB.11: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
WEB.12: So then, my beloved, even as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
WEB.13: For it is God who works in you both to will and to work, for his good pleasure.
WEB.14: Do all things without murmurings and disputes,
WEB.15: that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without defect in the middle of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you are seen as lights in the world,
WEB.16: holding up the word of life; that I may have something to boast in the day of Christ, that I didn’t run in vain nor labor in vain.
WEB.17: Yes, and if I am poured out on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice, and rejoice with you all.
WEB.18: In the same way, you also rejoice, and rejoice with me.
WEB.19: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, that I also may be cheered up when I know how you are doing.
WEB.20: For I have no one else like-minded, who will truly care about you.
WEB.21: For they all seek their own, not the things of Jesus Christ.
WEB.22: But you know the proof of him, that, as a child serves a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Good News.
WEB.23: Therefore I hope to send him at once, as soon as I see how it will go with me.
WEB.24: But I trust in the Lord that I myself also will come shortly.
WEB.25: But I counted it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, fellow soldier, and your apostle and servant of my need;
WEB.26: since he longed for you all, and was very troubled, because you had heard that he was sick.
WEB.27: For indeed he was sick, nearly to death, but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, that I might not have sorrow on sorrow.
WEB.28: I have sent him therefore the more diligently, that, when you see him again, you may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.
WEB.29: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such in honor,
WEB.30: because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to supply that which was lacking in your service toward me.

CSB17.1: If then there is any encouragement in Christ, if any consolation of love, <sup>ⓓ</sup> if any fellowship with the Spirit, <sup>ⓔ</sup> if any affection and mercy, <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: make my joy complete by thinking the same way, <sup>ⓖ</sup> having the same love, <sup>ⓗ</sup> united in spirit, intent on one <sup>ⓘ</sup> purpose.
CSB17.3: Do nothing out of selfish ambition <sup>ⓙ</sup> or conceit, <sup>ⓚ</sup> but in humility <sup>ⓛ</sup> consider others as more important <sup>ⓜ</sup> than yourselves.
CSB17.4: Everyone should look out not only for his own interests, <sup>ⓝ</sup> but also for the interests of others. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.5: Adopt the same attitude as that of Christ Jesus,
CSB17.6:
who, existing in the form of God,
did not consider equality with God ⓑ
as something to be exploited. [1]
CSB17.7:
Instead he emptied himself ⓒ
by assuming the form of a servant, ⓓ
taking on the likeness of humanity. ⓔ
And when he had come as a man,
CSB17.8:
he humbled himself by becoming obedient
to the point of death —
even to death on a cross. ⓕ
CSB17.9:
For this reason God highly exalted him ⓖ
and gave him the name
that is above every name,
CSB17.10:
so that at the name of Jesus
every knee will bow ⓗ  —
in heaven ⓘ and on earth
and under the earth ⓙ  —
CSB17.11:
and every tongue ⓚ will confess
that Jesus Christ is Lord,
to the glory ⓛ of God the Father.
CSB17.12: Therefore, my dear friends, <sup>ⓜ</sup> just as you have always obeyed, <sup>ⓝ</sup> so now, not only in my presence but even more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
CSB17.13: For it is God who is working in you both to will and to work according to his good purpose.
CSB17.14: Do everything without grumbling <sup>ⓐ</sup> and arguing, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: so that you may be blameless <sup>ⓒ</sup> and pure, <sup>ⓓ</sup> children of God who are faultless <sup>ⓔ</sup> in a crooked <sup>ⓕ</sup> and perverted <sup>ⓖ</sup> generation, <sup>ⓗ</sup> among whom you shine like stars in the world,
CSB17.16: by holding firm to the word <sup>ⓘ</sup> of life. Then I can boast <sup>ⓙ</sup> in the day of Christ <sup>ⓚ</sup> that I didn’t run or labor for nothing.
CSB17.17: But even if I am poured out <sup>ⓛ</sup> as a drink offering <sup>ⓜ</sup> on the sacrificial service of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with all of you.
CSB17.18: In the same way you should also be glad and rejoice with me.
CSB17.19: Now I hope in the Lord Jesus <sup>ⓝ</sup> to send Timothy <sup>ⓐ</sup> to you soon so that I too may be encouraged by news about you.
CSB17.20: For I have no one else like-minded who will genuinely care about your interests;
CSB17.21: all seek their own interests, <sup>ⓑ</sup> not those of Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.22: But you know his proven character, because he has served with me in the gospel ministry like a son with a father.
CSB17.23: Therefore, I hope to send him as soon as I see how things go with me.
CSB17.24: I am confident in the Lord that I myself will also come soon. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.25: But I considered it necessary to send you Epaphroditus <sup>ⓔ</sup>  — my brother, coworker, and fellow soldier, as well as your messenger and minister to my need <sup>ⓕ</sup>  —
CSB17.26: since he has been longing for all of you and was distressed because you heard that he was sick.
CSB17.27: Indeed, he was so sick that he nearly died. However, God had mercy on him, and not only on him but also on me, so that I would not have sorrow upon sorrow.
CSB17.28: For this reason, I am very eager to send him so that you may rejoice again when you see him and I may be less anxious.
CSB17.29: Therefore, welcome him in the Lord with great joy and hold people like him in honor,
CSB17.30: because he came close to death for the work <sup>ⓖ</sup> of Christ, risking his life to make up what was lacking in your ministry to me.

AMP.1: Therefore if there is any encouragement and comfort in Christ [as there certainly is in abundance], if there is any consolation of love, if there is any fellowship [that we share] in the Spirit, if [there is] any [great depth of] affection and compassion,
AMP.2: make my joy complete by being of the same mind, having the same love [toward one another], knit together in spirit, intent on one purpose [and living a life that reflects your faith and spreads the gospel—the good news regarding salvation through faith in Christ].
AMP.3: Do nothing from selfishness or empty conceit [through factional motives, or strife], but with [an attitude of] humility [being neither arrogant nor self-righteous], regard others as more important than yourselves.
AMP.4: Do not merely look out for your own personal interests, but also for the interests of others.
AMP.5: Have this same attitude in yourselves which was in Christ Jesus [look to Him as your example in selfless humility],
AMP.6: who, although He existed in the form and unchanging essence of God [as One with Him, possessing the fullness of all the divine attributes—the entire nature of deity], did not regard equality with God a thing to be grasped or asserted [as if He did not already possess it, or was afraid of losing it];
AMP.7: but emptied Himself [without renouncing or diminishing His deity, but only temporarily giving up the outward expression of divine equality and His rightful dignity] by assuming the form of a bond-servant, and being made in the likeness of men [He became completely human but was without sin, being fully God and fully man].
AMP.8: After He was found in [terms of His] outward appearance as a man [for a divinely-appointed time], He humbled Himself [still further] by becoming obedient [to the Father] to the point of death, even death on a cross.
AMP.9: For this reason also [because He obeyed and so completely humbled Himself], God has highly exalted Him and bestowed on Him the name which is above every name,
AMP.10: so that at the name of Jesusevery knee shall bow [in submission], of those who are in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
AMP.11: and that every tongue will confess and openly acknowledge that Jesus Christ is Lord (sovereign God), to the glory of God the Father.
AMP.12: So then, my dear ones, just as you have always obeyed [my instructions with enthusiasm], not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence, continue to work out your salvation [that is, cultivate it, bring it to full effect, actively pursue spiritual maturity] with awe-inspired fear and trembling [using serious caution and critical self-evaluation to avoid anything that might offend God or discredit the name of Christ].
AMP.13: For it is [not your strength, but it is] God who is effectively at work in you, both to will and to work [that is, strengthening, energizing, and creating in you the longing and the ability to fulfill your purpose] for His good pleasure.
AMP.14: Do everything without murmuring or questioning [the providence of God],
AMP.15: so that you may prove yourselves to be blameless and guileless, innocent and uncontaminated, children of God without blemish in the midst of a [morally] crooked and [spiritually] perverted generation, among whom you are seen as bright lights [beacons shining out clearly] in the world [of darkness],
AMP.16: holding out and offering to everyone the word of life, so that in the day of Christ I will have reason to rejoice greatly because I did not run [my race] in vain nor labor without result.
AMP.17: But even if I am being poured out as a drink offering on the sacrifice and service of your faith [for preaching the message of salvation], still I rejoice and share my joy with you all.
AMP.18: You too, rejoice in the same way and share your joy with me.
AMP.19: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I may also be encouraged by learning news about you.
AMP.20: For I have no one else [like him who is] so kindred a spirit who will be genuinely concerned for your [spiritual] welfare.
AMP.21: For the others [who deserted me after my arrest] all seek [to advance] their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
AMP.22: But you know of Timothy's tested worth and his proven character, that he has served with me to advance the gospel like a son serving with his father.
AMP.23: Therefore, I hope [that it is His will] to send him immediately, just as soon as I see how my case turns out;
AMP.24: and I trust [confidently] in the Lord that soon I also will be coming to you.
AMP.25: However, I thought it necessary to send back to you Epaphroditus, [who has been] my brother and companion and fellow soldier, who was also sent as your messenger to take care of my needs.
AMP.26: For he has been longing for all of you and was distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
AMP.27: He certainly was sick and close to death. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but also on me, so that I would not have sorrow upon sorrow.
AMP.28: So I have sent him all the more eagerly so that when you see him again you may rejoice and I may be less concerned about you.
AMP.29: Welcome him home in the Lord with great joy, and appreciate and honor men like him;
AMP.30: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to complete what was lacking in your service to me [which distance prevented you from rendering personally].

MSG.1: If you've gotten anything at all out of following Christ, if his love has made any difference in your life, if being in a community of the Spirit means anything to you, if you have a heart, if you care—
MSG.2: then do me a favor: Agree with each other, love each other, be deep-spirited friends.
MSG.3: Don't push your way to the front; don't sweet-talk your way to the top. Put yourself aside, and help others get ahead.
MSG.4: Don't be obsessed with getting your own advantage. Forget yourselves long enough to lend a helping hand.
MSG.5: Think of yourselves the way Christ Jesus thought of himself.
MSG.6: He had equal status with God but didn't think so much of himself that he had to cling to the advantages of that status no matter what.
MSG.7: Not at all. When the time came, he set aside the privileges of deity and took on the status of a slave, became human!
MSG.8: Having become human, he stayed human. It was an incredibly humbling process. He didn't claim special privileges. Instead, he lived a selfless, obedient life and then died a selfless, obedient death—and the worst kind of death at that: a crucifixion.
MSG.9: Because of that obedience, God lifted him high and honored him far beyond anyone or anything, ever,
MSG.10: so that all created beings in heaven and on earth—even those long ago dead and buried—will bow in worship before this Jesus Christ,
MSG.11: and call out in praise that he is the Master of all, to the glorious honor of God the Father.
MSG.12: What I'm getting at, friends, is that you should simply keep on doing what you've done from the beginning. When I was living among you, you lived in responsive obedience. Now that I'm separated from you, keep it up. Better yet, redouble your efforts. Be energetic in your life of salvation, reverent and sensitive before God.
MSG.13: That energy is God's energy, an energy deep within you, God himself willing and working at what will give him the most pleasure.
MSG.14: Do everything readily and cheerfully—no bickering, no second-guessing allowed!
MSG.15: Go out into the world uncorrupted, a breath of fresh air in this squalid and polluted society. Provide people with a glimpse of good living and of the living God. Carry the light-giving Message into the night
MSG.16: so I'll have good cause to be proud of you on the day that Christ returns. You'll be living proof that I didn't go to all this work for nothing.
MSG.17: Even if I am executed here and now, I'll rejoice in being an element in the offering of your faith that you make on Christ's altar, a part of your rejoicing.
MSG.18: But turnabout's fair play—you must join me in my rejoicing. Whatever you do, don't feel sorry for me.
MSG.19: I plan (according to Jesus' plan) to send Timothy to you very soon so he can bring back all the news of you he can gather. Oh, how that will do my heart good!
MSG.20: I have no one quite like Timothy. He is loyal, and genuinely concerned for you.
MSG.21: Most people around here are looking out for themselves, with little concern for the things of Jesus.
MSG.22: But you know yourselves that Timothy's the real thing. He's been a devoted son to me as together we've delivered the Message.
MSG.23: As soon as I see how things are going to fall out for me here, I plan to send him off.
MSG.24: And then I'm hoping and praying to be right on his heels.
MSG.25: But for right now, I'm dispatching Epaphroditus, my good friend and companion in my work. You sent him to help me out; now I'm sending him to help you out.
MSG.26: He has been wanting in the worst way to get back with you. Especially since recovering from the illness you heard about, he's been wanting to get back and reassure you that he is just fine.
MSG.27: He nearly died, as you know, but God had mercy on him. And not only on him—he had mercy on me, too. His death would have been one huge grief piled on top of all the others.
MSG.28: So you can see why I'm so delighted to send him on to you. When you see him again, hale and hearty, how you'll rejoice and how relieved I'll be.
MSG.29: Give him a grand welcome, a joyful embrace! People like him deserve the best you can give.
MSG.30: Remember the ministry to me that you started but weren't able to complete? Well, in the process of finishing up that work, he put his life on the line and nearly died doing it.

NIV.1:
Imitating Christ’s Humility
If you have any encouragement from being united with Christ, if any comfort from his love, if any fellowship with the Spirit, if any tenderness and compassion,
NIV.2: then make my joy complete by being like-minded, having the same love, being one in spirit and purpose.
NIV.3: Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit, but in humility consider others better than yourselves.
NIV.4: Each of you should look not only to your own interests, but also to the interests of others.
NIV.5: Your attitude should be the same as that of Christ Jesus:
NIV.6:
Who, being in very nature God,
did not consider equality with God something to be grasped,
NIV.7:
but made himself nothing,
taking the very nature of a servant,
being made in human likeness.
NIV.8:
And being found in appearance as a man,
he humbled himself
and became obedient to death—
even death on a cross!
NIV.9:
Therefore God exalted him to the highest place
and gave him the name that is above every name,
NIV.10:
that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow,
in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
NIV.11:
and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord,
to the glory of God the Father.
NIV.12:
Shining as Stars
Therefore, my dear friends, as you have always obeyed—not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence—continue to work out your salvation with fear and trembling,
NIV.13: for it is God who works in you to will and to act according to his good purpose.
NIV.14: Do everything without complaining or arguing,
NIV.15: so that you may become blameless and pure, children of God without fault in a crooked and depraved generation, in which you shine like stars in the universe
NIV.16: as you hold out the word of life—in order that I may boast on the day of Christ that I did not run or labor for nothing.
NIV.17: But even if I am being poured out like a drink offering on the sacrifice and service coming from your faith, I am glad and rejoice with all of you.
NIV.18: So you too should be glad and rejoice with me.
NIV.19:
Timothy and Epaphroditus
I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, that I also may be cheered when I receive news about you.
NIV.20: I have no one else like him, who takes a genuine interest in your welfare.
NIV.21: For everyone looks out for his own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
NIV.22: But you know that Timothy has proved himself, because as a son with his father he has served with me in the work of the gospel.
NIV.23: I hope, therefore, to send him as soon as I see how things go with me.
NIV.24: And I am confident in the Lord that I myself will come soon.
NIV.25: But I think it is necessary to send back to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker and fellow soldier, who is also your messenger, whom you sent to take care of my needs.
NIV.26: For he longs for all of you and is distressed because you heard he was ill.
NIV.27: Indeed he was ill, and almost died. But God had mercy on him, and not on him only but also on me, to spare me sorrow upon sorrow.
NIV.28: Therefore I am all the more eager to send him, so that when you see him again you may be glad and I may have less anxiety.
NIV.29: Welcome him in the Lord with great joy, and honor men like him,
NIV.30: because he almost died for the work of Christ, risking his life to make up for the help you could not give me.

NRSVCE.1: If then there is any encouragement in Christ, any consolation from love, any sharing in the Spirit, any compassion and sympathy,
NRSVCE.2: make my joy complete: be of the same mind, having the same love, being in full accord and of one mind.
NRSVCE.3: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility regard others as better than yourselves.
NRSVCE.4: Let each of you look not to your own interests, but to the interests of others.
NRSVCE.5: Let the same mind be in you that was in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.6:
who, though he was in the form of God,
did not regard equality with God
as something to be exploited,
NRSVCE.7:
but emptied himself,
taking the form of a slave,
being born in human likeness.
And being found in human form,
NRSVCE.8:
he humbled himself
and became obedient to the point of death —
even death on a cross.
NRSVCE.9:
Therefore God also highly exalted him
and gave him the name
that is above every name,
NRSVCE.10:
so that at the name of Jesus
every knee should bend,
in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
NRSVCE.11:
and every tongue should confess
that Jesus Christ is Lord,
to the glory of God the Father.
NRSVCE.12: Therefore, my beloved, just as you have always obeyed me, not only in my presence, but much more now in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling;
NRSVCE.13: for it is God who is at work in you, enabling you both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
NRSVCE.14: Do all things without murmuring and arguing,
NRSVCE.15: so that you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, in which you shine like stars in the world.
NRSVCE.16: It is by your holding fast to the word of life that I can boast on the day of Christ that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
NRSVCE.17: But even if I am being poured out as a libation over the sacrifice and the offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with all of you —
NRSVCE.18: and in the same way you also must be glad and rejoice with me.
NRSVCE.19: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I may be cheered by news of you.
NRSVCE.20: I have no one like him who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
NRSVCE.21: All of them are seeking their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.22: But Timothy’s worth you know, how like a son with a father he has served with me in the work of the gospel.
NRSVCE.23: I hope therefore to send him as soon as I see how things go with me;
NRSVCE.24: and I trust in the Lord that I will also come soon.
NRSVCE.25: Still, I think it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus — my brother and co-worker and fellow soldier, your messenger and minister to my need;
NRSVCE.26: for he has been longing for all of you, and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
NRSVCE.27: He was indeed so ill that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but on me also, so that I would not have one sorrow after another.
NRSVCE.28: I am the more eager to send him, therefore, in order that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.
NRSVCE.29: Welcome him then in the Lord with all joy, and honor such people,
NRSVCE.30: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to make up for those services that you could not give me.

NET.1: Therefore, if there is any encouragement in Christ, any comfort provided by love, any fellowship in the Spirit, any affection or mercy,
NET.2: complete my joy and be of the same mind, by having the same love, being united in spirit, and having one purpose.
NET.3: Instead of being motivated by selfish ambition or vanity, each of you should, in humility, be moved to treat one another as more important than yourself.
NET.4: Each of you should be concerned not only about your own interests, but about the interests of others as well.
NET.5: You should have the same attitude toward one another that Christ Jesus had,
NET.6: who though he existed in the form of God did not regard equality with God as something to be grasped,
NET.7: but emptied himself by taking on the form of a slave, by looking like other men, and by sharing in human nature.
NET.8: He humbled himself, by becoming obedient to the point of death — even death on a cross!
NET.9: As a result God exalted him and gave him the name that is above every name,
NET.10: so that at the name of Jesus every knee will bow — in heaven and on earth and under the earth —
NET.11: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord to the glory of God the Father.
NET.12: So then, my dear friends, just as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence but even more in my absence, continue working out your salvation with awe and reverence,
NET.13: for the one bringing forth in you both the desire and the effort — for the sake of his good pleasure — is God.
NET.14: Do everything without grumbling or arguing,
NET.15: so that you may be blameless and pure, children of God without blemish though you live in a crooked and perverse society, in which you shine as lights in the world
NET.16: by holding on to the word of life so that on the day of Christ I will have a reason to boast that I did not run in vain nor labor in vain.
NET.17: But even if I am being poured out like a drink offering on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I am glad and rejoice together with all of you.
NET.18: And in the same way you also should be glad and rejoice together with me.
NET.19: Now I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I too may be encouraged by hearing news about you.
NET.20: For there is no one here like him who will readily demonstrate his deep concern for you.
NET.21: Others are busy with their own concerns, not those of Jesus Christ.
NET.22: But you know his qualifications, that like a son working with his father, he served with me in advancing the gospel.
NET.23: So I hope to send him as soon as I know more about my situation,
NET.24: though I am confident in the Lord that I too will be coming to see you soon.
NET.25: But for now I have considered it necessary to send Epaphroditus to you. For he is my brother, coworker and fellow soldier, and your messenger and minister to me in my need.
NET.26: Indeed, he greatly missed all of you and was distressed because you heard that he had been ill.
NET.27: In fact he became so ill that he nearly died. But God showed mercy to him — and not to him only, but also to me — so that I would not have grief on top of grief.
NET.28: Therefore I am all the more eager to send him, so that when you see him again you can rejoice and I can be free from anxiety.
NET.29: So welcome him in the Lord with great joy, and honor people like him,
NET.30: since it was because of the work of Christ that he almost died. He risked his life so that he could make up for your inability to serve me.
